title: Delta Air 1Q misses Street estimates but stock flies as it offers a bullish outlook on travel demand
text: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) Inc's first-quarter earnings missed Wall Street estimates but investors looked past that as the company forecast higher-than-expected profit for the second quarter, citing “record” bookings for summer travel. The Atlanta-based airline said it expects 2Q earnings of $2 to $2.25 per share and continues to expect 2023 earnings of $5 to $6 per share.

title: Airline stocks get a broad boost from Delta's earnings, ‘strong' outlook
text: Shares of airline companies traded broadly higher Thursday, after Delta Air Lines Inc. beat first-quarter revenue expectations and provided a “strong outlook” for the current quarter. The U.S. Global Jets exchange-traded fund JETS, -3.12% rose 0.8% in premarket trading, as Delta's stock DAL, -2.43% jumped 2.5%.

title: Delta stock surges nearly 4% after revenue rose above expectations, while profit came up shy
text: Shares of Delta Air Lines Inc. DAL surged 3.7% in premarket trading Thursday, after the air carrier reported first-quarter profit that missed expectations but revenue that beat, and said it was “confident” in its full-year outlook. The company reported a net loss that narrowed to $363 million, or 57 cents a share, from $940 million, or $1.48 a share, in the year-ago period.
